assumption_text: The civilian denominator uses the built-up urban footprint of Grozny under shelling and assault, not all of Chechnya.
rationale: The battle was city-level and a whole-republic civilian denominator would mechanically understate civilian death risk.

assumption_text: The military denominator includes combatants inside Grozny or directly assigned to the city assault/encirclement from the immediate approaches; broader republic-wide forces are excluded.
rationale: Using the entire Russian force in Chechnya would violate the project's theater rule and dilute combatant exposure.

assumption_text: Start-of-battle civilian stock is modeled as 200,000 / 300,000 / 400,000 across low/best/high scenarios.
rationale: These scenarios bracket Hansen's 1989 population figure, the documented prewar Slav exodus, and Hansen's separate pre-war population reference of 400,000.

assumption_text: Mid-January civilian stock is anchored at 100,000, derived from Hansen's statement that one-quarter of Grozny's pre-war population of 400,000 remained behind.
rationale: This is the clearest in-battle civilian presence anchor for the city.

assumption_text: End-of-episode civilian stock is anchored at 30,000 in early March.
rationale: House of Commons quotes Fred Cuny that about 30,000 mostly elderly ethnic Russians remained in the rubble of the city by early March.

assumption_text: Combatant person-months are modeled in two phases: 12,000 / 20,000 / 33,800 assigned combatants in phase 1 and 7,000 / 12,000 / 18,500 in phase 2.
rationale: These scenarios blend Oliker's 6,000-man assault force and 1,000-10,000 defender range with Thomas's 23,800 operation-start pool, 38,000 wider operation ceiling, and 3,500 fighters still in the city on 21 January.

assumption_text: Unknown-status deaths are strictly excluded from the core DR rather than proportionally reallocated.
rationale: Accessible sources do not provide a clean unknown-status death series; strict exclusion avoids silently inflating either civilians or combatants.

assumption_text: The combatant-death best and high estimates add a conservative modeled Chechen fighter-fatality component to the observed Russian KIA.
rationale: Russian KIA are observable; Chechen battle deaths are not. A zero-addition point estimate would mechanically bias combatant mortality downward.

assumption_text: Person-months are computed by linear interpolation between civilian and combatant stock anchors using 30.4375 days per month.
rationale: No day-by-day population series exists, so piecewise-linear interpolation is the least aggressive practical approach.

assumption_text: The analytical aggregate episode ends on 1995-03-07, when Russian forces could declare full control over Grozny, even though some narratives use 1995-01-26 or around 1995-02-26 as battle endpoints.
rationale: This endpoint is conservative and aligns with the final city-capture framing in Gott while still acknowledging Thomas's earlier and later phase markers.
